description:
macFUSE 5.2's libfuse3 (announced as 3.18.2) on Apple platforms requires (1) extra Apple-only fields in fuse_operations to keep callback offsets aligned, AND (2) Darwin-flavored argument types for several callbacks. Without (1) the daemon mis-dispatches every callback past getattr (e.g. an OPEN opcode lands in our fuse_read with garbage size/offset). Without (2) the data we hand back is misinterpreted by macFUSE.

Status after the work in this branch:

  * fuse_operations layout fixed (4 Apple-only fields added: setattr, chflags,
    setvolname, monitor; struct is now 376 bytes / 47 fields). Layout tests
    cover the new offsets. Tracked separately in 4vl.7-prep work.

  * readdir slot stays null on macFUSE FSKit (workaround 1) — under FSKit the
    filler argument is an opaque token, not a function pointer, so calling it
    is unconditional UB. With the slot null, libfuse simply returns an empty
    listing for ls — directory enumeration won't work but doesn't crash.

  * Open/read/lookup/getattr now reach the right callbacks. But the next
    layer hits us: macFUSE's getattr expects to fill 'struct fuse_darwin_attr'
    (defined in /usr/local/include/fuse3/fuse_common.h around line 897), not
    POSIX 'struct stat'. Layouts diverge:

      fuse_darwin_attr           libc::stat (Darwin)
        ino_t  ino  @ 0            dev_t   st_dev   @ 0
        mode_t mode @ 8            mode_t  st_mode  @ 4
        nlink_t nlink @ 10         nlink_t st_nlink @ 6
        uid_t  uid  @ 12           ino_t   st_ino   @ 8

    Our fill_dir_stat() writes st_ino at offset 8 — macFUSE reads that as
    mode/nlink. Result: macFUSE thinks our root has ino 0; first GETATTR for
    the looked-up node aborts with 'fuse internal error: node 0 not found'.

Remaining work to make FSKit usable:

  1. Mirror struct fuse_darwin_attr in bindings.rs (#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]).
  2. Refactor fuse_getattr and fill_dir_stat (and any other spot that fills a
     stat-shaped buffer) to populate fuse_darwin_attr on macOS.
  3. Investigate whether FSKit will actually enumerate directories via
     LOOKUP-per-entry once the data is correct, or whether we need a properly
     macFUSE-compatible readdir filler. Both bundled appex variants
     (.macfuse vs .macfuse-local) deserve a try.
  4. Verify other Darwin-extended callbacks (statfs uses statfs not statvfs,
     setxattr/getxattr have an extra uint32_t options arg, utimens takes
     timespec[3]). For unimplemented callbacks (statfs/setxattr/getxattr we
     have stubs/None) the signature mismatch may not bite, but verify.

Until 4vl.6 lands, the daemon is unable to serve a working FSKit mount.
Reverting the -o backend=fskit option restores macFUSE's kext fallback, but
that path is blocked on Apple Silicon Tahoe by syspolicyd unless the user
enables Reduced Security mode in Recovery.
title: fuse_operations needs full Apple/Darwin port for FSKit
